name: Descope
firstSuccessOutcome: A React app runs a Descope Flow that signs up and logs in a user, creating a session.

steps.action: Open Descope getting-started quickstart docs
Sign up for free Descope account
Verify email to activate account
Create project, copy Project ID
Install @descope/react-sdk via npm
Wrap app in AuthProvider with Project ID
Add Descope sign-up-or-in Flow component
Run app and open the login flow
Complete login; Descope session established

name: F5 Distributed Cloud
routeName: Get Started with Distributed Cloud Console
firstSuccessOutcome: You log into the Distributed Cloud Console and reach your tenant workspaces.

steps.action: Open F5 Distributed Cloud Console quickstart
Sign up for free Distributed Cloud account
Verify email; receive tenant setup instructions
Set password via emailed update link
Go to Console portal, enter tenant name
Log in with SaaS account credentials
Accept terms and conditions to proceed
Choose work domains and skill level
View workspaces on the Console home page

name: GitGuardian
firstSuccessOutcome: ggshield scans a repository and reports detected hardcoded secret incidents.

steps.action: Open GitGuardian ggshield getting-started guide
Create free GitGuardian workspace account
Verify email to activate workspace
Install ggshield via install script, brew, or pipx
Run ggshield auth login and authorize in browser
Run ggshield secret scan repo /path
Review detected secret incidents in output

name: Ory
firstSuccessOutcome: You register a user through Ory's hosted flow and receive an authenticated session.

steps.action: Open Ory Network local development docs
Sign up for free Ory Network account
Install Ory CLI via curl, brew, or scoop
Authenticate the CLI to your Ory account
Run ory create project to provision a project
Start Ory Tunnel to localhost
Open registration flow at localhost:4000/ui/registration
Register a user; session cookie created

name: Stytch
firstSuccessOutcome: A Stytch login component authenticates a user and returns an active session.

steps.action: Open Stytch consumer quickstart docs
Sign up for free Stytch account
Verify email to activate account
Create project, copy public_token from dashboard
Enable Frontend SDKs, add authorized domain
Install @stytch/react SDK via npm
Wrap app in StytchProvider with public_token
Add StytchLogin component with products config
Run app and open the login page
Complete login; Stytch session active

name: Teleport
firstSuccessOutcome: Your hosted Teleport cluster launches and you sign into its web UI.

steps.action: Open Teleport get-started deploy-cloud guide
Go to goteleport.com/signup, start free trial
Verify email via activation link
Follow sign-up steps to launch cluster
Set up credentials and MFA device
Save generated recovery codes securely
Log into your example.teleport.sh cluster
Reach the Teleport web UI dashboard

name: Twingate
firstSuccessOutcome: You reach a private resource through Twingate from any network via the Client.

steps.action: Open Twingate Quick Start guide
Sign up for free Twingate account
Complete questionnaire, choose network name and URL
Create a Remote Network in admin console
Add a Connector and generate deployment tokens
Run Connector deploy command on a host
Define a Resource by private IP or DNS
Install Twingate Client from get.twingate.com
Authenticate the Client to your network
Access the private Resource from your device
